summary: Engineer Abd Al-Ghafour Khan presents an 18-year research project exploring the miracles of the Quran through the lens of modern physics and science. He begins by discussing Quranic verses about not distinguishing between divine religions and their common message of monotheism and justice. The core of his presentation focuses on Quran 51:49 ("And of everything We have created pairs"), which he connects to scientific discoveries about particle physics, quantum mechanics, and the fundamental symmetry of the universe. He explains how the concept of pairing exists at all levels—from atoms and subatomic particles to antimatter and beyond—and relates this to the Quranic concept of balance (Mizan) and natural justice.
suggested_title: The Miracles of the Quran: Divine Pairs and Universal Balance in Light of Modern Physics
catchy_title: Did the Quran describe particle physics?
